page: Microsoft Azure E48bs_v5 is an EBSv5-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 24 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores operating at 2.8 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512, and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. No local storage or GPUs are included, but the instance is Premium Storage capable for external block storage.

Benchmark results indicate strong memory and database performance. The instance achieved a strong PassMark memory score of 2814.85 and a database operations score of 23477.08. Large block memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 259.6 GB/sec for read-only and 128.15 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-core CPU performance is solid, with a PassMark CPU score of 50828.28 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 46616.21. Static web serving throughput is strong at 45.29 GB/sec. However, LLM inference on large 70B models is a bottleneck, yielding a poor text generation speed of 2.41 tokens/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E48bs_v5 offers a dense memory profile and high remote storage performance without the overhead of local NVMe drives. This makes it a qualitatively cost-effective option for memory-intensive workloads that rely on external premium block storage. The server is highly suited for large relational databases, enterprise applications, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and data processing pipelines.
description: Microsoft Azure E48bs_v5 is an EBSv5-family virtual machine configured with 48 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, utilizing the x86_64 architecture with Intel Xeon 8370C processors running at 2.8 GHz. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 24 physical cores with hyper-threading and lacks local storage, relying instead on Premium Storage block capabilities. Performance benchmarks highlight strong capabilities in memory bandwidth, database operations, and static web serving throughput, alongside a robust PassMark CPU score of 50828.28. Conversely, large-scale LLM inference represents a performance tradeoff, with poor text generation speeds on 70B models. Qualitatively, the server provides a resource-dense memory profile optimized for external storage workloads. It is best suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, and enterprise workloads requiring high memory capacity and strong block storage performance.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E48bs_v5 is an EBSv5 virtual machine featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB of RAM, and premium block storage capabilities, optimized for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E48bs_v5 is an EBSv5 virtual machine with 48 Intel vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-intensive and database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high memory bandwidth and premium block storage capabilities for demanding database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 8370C processors at 2.8 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core
Optimized for Premium Storage block performance with no local storage
Strong database operations and large-block memory bandwidth benchmarks
Suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and enterprise applications
